Xandrio · TTS engine bench · July 2026

Four engines, one paragraph, your voice.

Every sample below speaks the same passage with the brick-scott cloned voice on the M4. Playing one pauses the others, so you can flip between them mid-sentence. RTF is generation time over audio length — under 1.0 means the engine outruns playback.

“Brother Francis Gerard of Utah might never have discovered the blessed documents, had it not been for the pilgrim with girded loins who appeared during that young novice’s Lenten fast in the desert…”

Chatterbox Original

PyTorch MPS — quality ceiling

RTF
9.42
Gen time
194 s
Memory
~5 GB

The voice you liked best — and why it was unusable: over 3 minutes to speak 20 seconds. The bar the others are judged against.

Chatterbox Turbo

PyTorch MPS — current server

RTF
1.51–3.5
Gen time
28–72 s
Memory
~4 GB

Today’s premium quality reference. Too slow to generate while you listen.

MLX fp16

Same weights, Apple-native runtime. Hovers around realtime; swings with thermals.

Proposed engine

Chatterbox Original

MLX 8-bit

RTF
0.54
Gen time
11.5 s
Memory
2.3 GB

The headline result: Original-model quality (CFG intact) at faster than realtime — 17× quicker than the PyTorch run above. New leading candidate.

Chatterbox Turbo

MLX 8-bit

RTF
0.46–0.49
Gen time
~9 s
Memory
2.8 GB

Quantized Turbo. Twice realtime, consistent across runs. Slightly faster than Original 8-bit; compare quality by ear.

Kokoro

am_onyx — instant engine

RTF
0.22
Gen time
4.1 s
Memory
small

The deep male Kokoro voice — the instant fallback paired with brick-scott while premium audio generates.

What to listen for between fp16 and 8-bit: voice timbre drift, sibilance (“s” sounds), and breath/pause naturalness — quantization artifacts show up there first. If 8-bit passes, background premium generation runs at twice realtime; if not, fp16 still roughly doubles the current server’s throughput. Note each generation samples fresh — pacing and small deliveries differ between takes; judge voice quality, not word-for-word identity.
